method: lane_history.py --since 385 minus S421+ lanes. Outcome labels from dispatch_optimizer.py --json current state. L/lane approximated from global historical domain rates.

STR.note: strategy domain fully resolved S404, now inactive

mixed_share_label_adjusted.note: META label changed MIXED->PROVEN between S385 and S420 (37 lanes reclassified). Adjusted treats meta as MIXED throughout for comparability.
mixed_share_label_adjusted.interpretation: With label continuity, MIXED share is STABLE (23%->26%). Apparent collapse to 4.1% is methodological artifact from meta reclassification.

l_per_lane_by_label.note: Estimated from global domain historical rates, not direct window measurement

s363_baseline.note: S363 L/lane values are from direct lesson counting (different methodology)

s385_baseline.note: S385 used current-era labels where meta=MIXED

notes: UCB1 equilibrium analysis: (1) META concentration grew from 18.2% (S415) to 21.9% (S420), continuing rich-get-richer effect. (2) MIXED dispatch effectively zero (4.1%) under current labels — but META was MIXED at S363/S385, and its reclassification to PROVEN accounts for most of the apparent collapse. (3) Label-adjusted MIXED share (26%) is near S385 level (23%), consistent with STABLE UCB1 behavior around MIXED domains. (4) UNLABELED at 42% vs S385's 37% — UCB1 continues dispersing into novel/inactive domains. (5) STRUGGLING = 0 for entire window: no domain labeled STRUGGLING in dispatch_optimizer. (6) Three UCB1 failure modes from L-927 persist: rich-get-richer (META 22%), log compression (explore term insufficient), score-to-behavior gap. Key finding: outcome-label dispatch quality is STABLE in label-corrected terms, not WORSENED. The measured MIXED collapse (62%->80%->23%->4%) is confounded by META label evolution from MIXED to PROVEN.
falsification_test: F-EXP10 original question was whether UCB1 reaches stable equilibrium for outcome-label dispatch. Evidence: MIXED share with label correction shows STABLE behavior around 23-26% since S385. UCB1 is NOT converging toward MIXED-dominant allocation (the optimal per S363 yield data). Equilibrium exists but at PROVEN-dominated setpoint, not MIXED-dominated.
